Warsh has been a vocal critic of the Fed’s massive asset purchase programs that expanded its balance sheet to about $6.7 trillion.

items.title: Global Market: Japanese investors turn net sellers of foreign stocks after four months
items.summary: Japanese investors became net sellers of foreign equities in April for the first time in four months, offloading 636.4 billion yen due to rising energy costs and inflation fears. Trust accounts led this sell-off, while investment trusts and life insurers continued to buy overseas stocks.  This shift occurred as U.S. inflation accelerated, reinforcing expectations of prolonged high interest rates.

items.title: This small-cap chemical stock rallies 20% after strong Q4 results. Do you own?

items.title: Honda books first annual loss, hit by $9 billion EV charge
items.summary: Honda Motor reported its first annual loss in almost 70 years, totaling 414.3 billion yen, due to U.S. tariffs and significant restructuring costs for its electric vehicle business. The automaker incurred over $9 billion in EV-related losses and anticipates further expenses, though it expects to return to profitability this fiscal year.

items.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed by Senate as 17th Federal Reserve chair
items.summary: The vote came just days before Jerome Powell’s term as chair expires Friday.

items.title: GRWM: Kids’ cosmetics brands vie to ‘catch them young’
items.summary: The 'get ready with me' trend has propelled children into the beauty industry, with 'kidfluencers' driving early engagement with cosmetics. While brands emphasize play, experts warn of potential social conditioning and the shift from experimentation to routine, raising concerns about self-image and external validation for young audiences.

items.title: Preparing for Rupee at 100: What does it mean for the economy and your stock market investments?
items.summary: The Indian rupee is nearing the Rs 100 per dollar mark, driven by rising crude oil prices and foreign investor outflows. This depreciation fuels imported inflation, complicates RBI policy, and creates uneven impacts across equity sectors. Investors are advised to focus on resilient businesses as currency volatility persists.

The company highlighted a stable, infrastructure-led business model poised for sustained cash generation and value creation.

items.title: NLC India share price zooms 15% after Q4 results 2026

items.title: Jhunjhunwala-backed Bagmane Prime Office REIT lists at 3.5% premium over IPO price

The Bengaluru-focused REIT, backed by Blackstone, attracted significant investor interest, with its portfolio boasting high occupancy and marquee tenants like Google and Amazon.

Pashupati Advani of Global Foray advises caution, highlighting opportunities in infrastructure, exporters, and pharma, while also suggesting that investors prioritize fundamentals and government spending priorities.

items.title: We are seeing momentum for Indian investment in Sri Lanka, lot of interest in hospitality, ports, logistics: Ceylon Chamber of Commerce Chairperson

Regional cooperation is highlighted as key to strengthening the area.

items.title: Bharti Airtel shares gain over 2% after Q4 results. Why Morgan Stanley sees more upside

Investors focused on strong operational performance, with revenue climbing 16% to Rs 55,383 crore. Growth was led by steady momentum in the India business and resilient Africa operations, while India revenue, including passive infrastructure services, rose 8% year-on-year.

items.title: Rupee hits record low of 95.85/USD as energy risk worries deepen
items.summary: The Indian rupee reached an all-time low on Thursday, driven by elevated oil prices and continuous foreign portfolio outflows. This has created strain on India's current and capital accounts, with the currency particularly vulnerable due to its heavy reliance on oil imports, especially if the Iran conflict persists.

items.title: India’s top banks failing to tackle climate risk as threats grow
items.summary: India's major banks are disclosing more climate data but not using it for lending decisions.  Physical climate risks like floods and heat are worsening, impacting bank portfolios.  Few banks conduct climate stress tests or phase out coal lending.  The Reserve Bank of India is providing data but not mandating disclosures.  Delayed action risks financial instability and stranded assets for banks.

items.summary: The US-Israel-Iran conflict has significantly boosted India's power and energy stocks, with Nifty Energy's market cap rising by Rs 3 lakh crore as Brent crude exceeds $100. Foreign investors are pouring into power generation and transmission, viewing it as a macroeconomic hedge. Companies like Adani Power and BHEL are leading the surge, while oil marketing companies face losses.

items.summary: Tata Motors (Commercial Vehicles) reported strong quarterly earnings with a sharp rise in net profit and revenue, but its stock fell by over 3% amid cautious sentiment on the commercial vehicle sector outlook. Brokerages remain split, with neutral to bullish views on future growth driven by exports and industry recovery.

items.title: How your portfolio diversification may turn into ‘di-worsification’? Ace investor Charlie Munger explains
items.summary: Munger argued that excessive diversification can dilute portfolio quality and reduce the impact of high-conviction investments. While diversification is traditionally considered a risk-management strategy, overdoing it may lead investors to allocate capital into mediocre businesses.

MCX silver futures for July 2026 fell Rs 3,359, or 1.1%, to Rs 2,96,879 per kg, while June 2026 gold futures dropped Rs 1,159, or 0.7%, to Rs 1,61,027 per 10 grams. The decline follows a strong rally in the previous session, when silver surged over Rs 21,000 and gold gained nearly Rs 9,000.

The ban applies to most shipments, with exceptions for specific quotas and ongoing consignments. This move impacts global sugar trade and India's position as a major exporter.

The estimated ₹40,000 crore pipeline will connect Oman to Gujarat. It is expected to take five to seven years to build. This move addresses India's energy security concerns and its vulnerability to maritime choke points.

items.title: The big screen’s back in spotlight as Rs 13,000 crore box-office revival fades OTT boom
items.summary: Indian cinema is back to prioritizing movie theaters. Films now stream much later after their big screen debut. This shift is driven by studios seeking box-office success. Streaming platforms are spending less on movie rights. This change benefits all involved in the industry. Box-office collections reached a record high in 2025.

items.summary: In a significant move for medical innovation, Impetis Biosciences, a subsidiary of Tata group, has teamed up with Vyome Holdings from the US to license two cutting-edge experimental treatments. Focusing on challenges like rheumatoid arthritis and hair thinning, these selective JAK inhibitors are engineered to mitigate risks tied to earlier treatments.

items.title: Zydus Lifesciences acquires US pharma firm Assertio for $166 million

The purchase includes Assertio’s essential cancer treatment, Rolvedon, and offers Zydus a rich commercial network to elevate its oncology initiatives.

items.title: Fuel prices likely to rise if war continues: RBI Guv
items.summary: "If this is to continue for longer period of time, it is just a matter of time before the government will pass on some of the price increases," Malhotra said at a conference hosted by the Swiss National Bank and the International Monetary Fund in Switzerland on Tuesday. Excise duties had been cut while state-run fuel retailers were absorbing the increase in crude prices as the conflict continues, he added.

The dialogue included vital topics such as enhancing transmission capacities and the potential of variable time-of-day pricing. These approaches are strategically aimed at amplifying the adoption of renewable energy and reinforcing the stability of the power grid.

items.title: Duty hike may bring out more old gold into market
items.summary: Industry executives said promoting sales of lower carat jewellery like 14K and 9K can reduce imports by 20-30%, and discouraging investments in gold bars and coins can reduce imports by another 20% -30%. India annually imports 750-800 tonnes of gold to meet its demand. Revamping the Gold Monetisation Scheme can also help the government to tap 25,000 tonnes of grandfather stock.

items.title: Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh top logistics performance ranking, Mizoram and Delhi also lead
items.summary: The LEADS 2025 report has unveiled the logistics champions of India, with Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Mizoram, and Delhi being hailed as 'exemplars'. Gujarat and Kerala are also making waves as 'high performers' among an elite group of 11 states, reflecting their exceptional efforts in logistics development.

items.title: Austerity drive: Duty hike pushes up bullion prices by 7%
items.summary: The government late Tuesday increased import duties on gold and silver to 15% from 6% with effect from May 13. The step is aimed at curbing overseas purchases of precious metals and reducing pressure on the country's foreign exchange reserves. The revised structure includes a 10% basic customs duty along with a 5% Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess (AIDC), lifting the effective import tax to 15%.

items.title: SpiceJet seeks Supreme Court approval to submit land title as security

The MSP for paddy (rice), the main kharif crop, has been increased by Rs 72 per quintal, taking it to Rs 2,441 for the 'common' variety and Rs 2,461 for 'Grade A'.

items.title: India’s emergence has a deficit that may not be too late for us to collectively close—empathy
items.summary: The gaping divide between the rich and poor in India is stark, especially in its cities like Bengaluru. This cannot be blamed solely on policy. What individuals see and choose not to see could make a significant difference to how millions of lives are lived.

items.title: GJEPC says higher gold import duty may raise prices, fuel smuggling and hurt MSME exporters
items.summary: While backing Narendra Modi’s broader call for reducing non-essential imports and supporting the economy, the industry body said higher duties have rarely reduced gold imports in proportion to rising prices.

items.title: Germany wants to put TikTok 'in European hands'
items.summary: The EU told TikTok in February that it needed to change its "addictive design" or risk heavy fines

items.title: RBI Governor sees fuel price hike if crude oil stays high
items.summary: His remarks came after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged voluntary austerity, including cutting down on petrol and diesel use and putting off gold purchases, to preserve foreign exchange reserves.

items.title: Gold, silver futures rally 7% after govt raises import duty on bullion
items.summary: The duty hike could moderate imports in the near term as the government looks to contain pressure on the current account deficit

items.title: Smaller Indian airlines cut international flights sharply amid West Asia war
items.summary: Air India Express, Akasa Air and SpiceJet have cut overseas departures by nearly 60% as Gulf route exposure and operational pressures weigh on smaller carriers, even as IndiGo and Air India see smaller reductions.

items.title: Missing lens: markets must put a price tag on the climate risks that companies face
items.summary: Indian markets react instantly to oil shocks, elections and earnings misses. Yet climate risk, one of the biggest long-term threats to corporate performance, barely affects how companies and assets are valued. This disconnect can’t survive contact with reality.

items.title: Sugar Cosmetics seeks  ₹100-150 crore rescue funding at steep valuation cut
items.summary: The beauty brand is seeking fresh funding from family offices, HNIs and existing investors as it battles a cash crunch and retraces its offline expansion.

items.title: Rupee recovers 16 paise from all-time low to 95.52 against U.S. dollar in early trade
items.summary: Meanwhile, the dollar index, which gauges the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, was trading at 98.30, up 0.01%

items.title: Malabar Gold & Diamonds seeks measures to support Gold Monetisation Scheme
items.summary: Company emphasises focus on recycling, exchange, re-use, and monetisation of existing domestic gold to reduce import dependency

items.title: Maruti Suzuki ramps up green logistics, targets 35% rail dispatch by 2031
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki has already increased rail share to 26.5%, crossing 30 lakh cumulative rail dispatches

items.title: Japan megabanks to gain access to Anthropic's Mythos in about two weeks, source says
items.summary: Mythos is viewed by ⁠cybersecurity experts as posing significant challenges to the banking industry and its legacy technology systems

items.title: Fuel price pass-through needs monitoring as weather risks also posing pressure on inflation: BoB report
items.summary: Rising fuel prices and weather uncertainties could push India's inflation higher in FY27, despite current low CPI levels. Bank of Baroda warns that global energy costs impacting food prices, coupled with domestic weather challenges like heatwaves and El Nino, necessitate close monitoring of food inflation. Core inflation remains stable, but vigilance is advised.

items.title: Streaming industry shifts to consolidation mode amid rising costs, competition and clutter
items.summary: The streaming industry is consolidating as smaller players face rising costs and competition. Experts suggest these moves indicate a shift towards strategic alliances and efficiency in content monetization and distribution.

items.title: Stock markets pare early gains; trade lower
items.summary: From the 30-Sensex firms, Power Grid, NTPC, Bajaj Finance, State Bank of India, Titan and Axis Bank were among the biggest laggards

items.title: Black Box completes acquisition of Brazilian company 2S Inovações Tecnológicas S.A.
items.summary: The acquisition, effective May 1, strengthens Black Box’s capabilities and market presence across Latin America, particularly in areas such as data center networking, digital connectivity, cybersecurity, and managed infrastructure

items.title: West Asia crisis yet to impact inflation, but fuel prices pass-through to shape outlook: Report
items.summary: India's retail inflation remains stable despite global tensions. Future price trends will depend on fuel price pass-through and summer crop coverage. Imported inflation has slightly eased. Restaurants and accommodation saw price rises due to LPG shortages. Softening gold and silver prices helped ease personal care inflation. Retail inflation is under control for now.

items.title: RBI likely to maintain rate pause as FY27 CPI seen at 4.8-4.9%: Report
items.summary: Elara Capital report flags upside risks from oil prices and rupee depreciation, with potential hike only if inflation hits 6 per cent durably

items.title: AERA defers 15% revenue recovery to moderate Noida airport charges
items.summary: Regulator says part of airport’s revenue requirement was shifted to next tariff cycle to reduce burden on passengers and airlines

items.title: Noida airport domestic flyers to pay ₹490 user development fee: AERA
items.summary: Tariff for greenfield facility to rise gradually till FY31 with UDF up to ₹1,461 for departing international passengers

items.title: TVS Credit Q4 net profit up 13% at ₹256 crore
items.summary: For the full year FY26, net profit after tax stood at ₹913 crore, a growth of 19% from FY25

items.title: Gold duty under India-UAE pact raised in line with standard rate hike
items.summary: Gold import under specified quota to now attract duties of 14 per cent instead of 5 per cent; experts advise tighter origin rules to check misuse

items.title: JBM Auto doubles down on electric vehicles push in strategic shift from auto components manufacture
items.summary: Board renames OEM Division as ‘EV Business’ as receivables jump 117%, cash flow drops 59%, and debt rises 61% amid rapid electric bus expansion

items.title: Gold has evolved beyond its traditional role—which may help us mobilize holdings for financial resilience
items.summary: In much of India, gold is an integral part of the household financial playbook. But the cultural context has been evolving in ways that make it easier to deploy vast private holdings—among the world’s biggest—for productive economic purposes.

items.title: Jio Platforms appoints Akash Ambani as managing director ahead of IPO
items.summary: In a key pre-IPO move, Jio Platforms has appointed Akash Ambani as managing director for five years, as the company moves closer to filing its DRHP with Sebi for a blockbuster listing.

items.title: Nestlé India launches Nespresso store in Mumbai
items.summary: Third outlet for the brand focuses on personalised coffee discovery and experience in Bandra Kurla Complex

items.title: Rising inflation, Hormuz deadlock, Trump-Xi meet: What’s driving global news headlines
items.summary: Inflation is flaring up again as the West Asia war disrupts energy flows and keeps oil above $100 amid a prolonged Strait of Hormuz standoff. The Trump-Xi meeting could reshape trade dynamics, while the 2026 World Cup promises a sizeable global growth boost.

items.title: India's energy illusion: why ambitious targets mean nothing without a coherent plan
items.summary: Soaring crude oil prices triggered by the Israel-US-Iran war are straining India's economy, exposing its heavy dependence on imported fossil fuels and a fragile energy strategy.&nbsp;

items.title: India's travel industry braces for hit from Modi's appeal to avoid foreign trips
items.summary: The travel pullback is unfolding against a backdrop of rising global inflation, with crude oil trading above $100 a barrel after Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz choked off a fifth of global supply of oil ​and liquefied natural gas

items.title: Oil prices ease after rising in three consecutive sessions, Brent still around $107/bbl
items.summary: Crude prices pause after an 8% surge, but the 75-day closure of the Strait of Hormuz keeps oil above $100, squeezing India and forcing tough choices as OMC losses swell.

items.title: India-Japan seek to advance coop in critical minerals, semiconductors & ICT
items.summary: India and Japan have strengthened economic ties. A key dialogue focused on industrial and technological cooperation. Five sectors were prioritised: critical minerals, semiconductors, ICT, clean energy, and pharmaceuticals. Both nations aim to build resilient supply chains. Discussions also covered defense, trade, and energy security.

items.title: Diaspora dollars: be thankful for non-residents sending money home but let’s not taken these flows for granted
items.summary: With India’s foreign-exchange reserves at $691 billion, we can breathe easy on the external front for now. Credit the Indian diaspora for some of this. But resident citizens, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged, must do their bit as well.

items.title: India’s economic strength defies foreign outflow fears, S&P Says
items.summary: S&P Global Ratings said India is coping with global financial pressures better than headline data indicate, adding that concerns over foreign investment outflows are overstated. The agency said India has enough buffers to manage a wider current-account deficit caused by rising oil prices.

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | India’s inflation incline could put rate hikes back in RBI’s policy consideration set
items.summary: India’s inflation may still be below the Reserve Bank of India’s 4% target, but is on an upward trajectory. Unless price pressures somehow ease, tighter monetary policy may only be a matter of time.

items.title: India books phosphate fertilizer at 40% above pre-war prices
items.summary: India has contracted diammonium phosphate (DAP) fertilizer at prices nearly 40% higher than pre-war levels as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and lifts global prices. Indian Potash Ltd. agreed to buy about 1.35 million tons of DAP for delivery on the west and east coasts at around $930-$935 per ton, exceeding the 1.2 million tons sought in last week’s tender.

items.title: India raises gold, silver import tariffs to 15%; revises customs duty on precious metal findings
items.summary: The Indian Government has raised effective import tariffs on gold and silver to 15%. The move aims to curb bullion imports and strengthen the Indian National Rupee against the US Dollar

items.title: Blackstone-backed Ventive doubles down on luxury hospitality with  ₹2,000 cr push
items.summary: With a  ₹2,000 crore expansion, Ventive Hospitality aims to capitalize on luxury travel demand. The firm operates 13 assets and plans to grow by focusing on large luxury projects and acquisitions in key markets like Goa, Sri Lanka and the Maldives.

items.title: Aditya Birla Health bets on wellness incentives to improve claims ratios
items.summary: Aditya Birla Health wants to reward healthier customers, nudge those on the edge to be more consistent, and, lastly, intervene with high-risk customers to help them manage their health.

items.title: A third legal salvo against Tata Trusts ahead of key 16 May meeting
items.summary: A legal notice has been sent to the trustees of Sir Ratan Tata Trust regarding the transfer of shares in Tata Sons from a small trust to Naval Tata, father of Noel Tata and the late Ratan Tata.

items.title: Auto Inc stares at stress in Bharat as signs of slowdown emerge
items.summary: India's largest tractor makers and a motorcycle company anticipate declining sales due to poor monsoon conditions, risking stress in the rural auto industry. Mahindra forecasts 5% growth, while Escorts expects a 2-3% decline.&nbsp;

items.title: Video pitch, assessment, socials scan—hiring goes beyond CVs
items.summary: The CV is clearly losing its position as the main document that decides who moves forward. Hiring teams now lean more on assessments, video introductions and digital footprints to judge skills, communication and workplace fit.

items.title: Actis restarts Nxt-Infra sale process, appoints Ambit
items.summary: Interest in road assets has been buoyant in India lately, driven by the availability of a large number of assets that attract large institutional investors, including pension and sovereign wealth funds.

items.title: Inflation in driver’s seat likely to stall discretionary spend
items.summary: While consumer demand had started improving over the past four months after a prolonged slowdown, rising crude oil prices, supply disruptions and Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent call for austerity could threaten to derail that recovery, said executives and analysts. The impact is expected to be sharpest in electronics.

items.title: Containing war impact on growth, current account: PMO stitching up plan to boost foreign fund flow
items.summary: Officials at both the finance ministry and Niti Aayog are identifying economic opportunities presented by the Iran conflict, as well as gauging the impact of the surge in global oil prices on the economy under different price bands. Niti Aayog will likely draw up a report on matter.

items.title: Tata Power Q4 PAT up 8%, revenue down 8%, dividend at ₹2.50 per share
items.summary: Praveer Sinha, CEO and Managing Director, Tata Power, said, “This quarter and year reflect our sustained focus on creating long-term value through disciplined growth, operational excellence and strategic partnerships”

items.title: GDP data release revised to June 7 each year
items.summary: The statistics ministry has adjusted the release date for provisional GDP estimates and fourth-quarter data to June 7 annually, moving from the last working day of May. This change, driven by data availability delays, will see FY26 provisional GDP and Q4FY26 data released on June 5.

items.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
items.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

items.title: Govt eyes new employment blueprint after labour code rollout
items.summary: The central government has fully operationalised four new labour codes, consolidating 29 laws into a simplified framework designed to be both pro-worker and business-friendly. These codes aim to foster effective coordination between labour and industry. The ministry also plans a comprehensive employment policy to guide future job generation.

items.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
items.summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

items.title: CEA Nageswaran says West Asia crisis a balance of payments stress test; to impact CAD, inflation & exchange rate
items.summary: Large companies must pay micro and small enterprises on time. This eases their working capital and reduces funding costs. Managing the current account, financing it, and preventing currency depreciation are key macroeconomic goals for FY27. The West Asia crisis presents a balance of payments stress test.

items.title: West Asia crisis a wake-up call for self-reliance: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: The West Asia crisis serves as a crucial reminder for Indian industries to boost self-reliance and efficiency. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged businesses to reduce import dependency and focus on indigenous design and manufacturing. He also highlighted the need for faster electric vehicle adoption to cut oil bills.

items.title: Delhi High Court seeks Centre’s stand on plea to broadcast FIFA World Cup
items.summary: No broadcaster has acquired the broadcasting rights this year for the FIFA World Cup, one of the most viewed sporting events in the world, the petition says

items.title: India-Oman FTA on track for June 1 start even as deal with Chile hits hurdles, says Commerce Minister
items.summary: The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement was signed in December 2025

items.title: EXIM Bank plans $10.5 billion debt fundraise in fiscal 2027
items.summary: It will raise about $3.5 billion from overseas markets ⁠via bilateral and syndicated loans

items.title: Adani Ports to invest $1.36 billion in Europe expansion
items.summary: As ⁠part of the planned expansion, its marine services unit Astra Offshore will develop ​a fleet ‌of 200 vessels with specialised offshore capabilities

items.title: The toll of structural adjustments on the global South and a case for accountability
items.summary: In the 1980s, IMF and World Bank put conditions on financing the global South. Decades later, many countries in the regions struggle with weak public health systems and high levels of poverty. The institutions owe reparations for the damage caused, a new paper argues.

items.title: New Income Tax law aims to build transparent, tech-driven ecosystem for taxpayers: Official
items.summary: Pune's Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, Vivek Wadekar, announced the new Income Tax Act, 2025, aims to simplify compliance and foster transparency. Launched on April 1, 2026, the act features digital initiatives like the 'Kar Saathi' chatbot and 'Kar Setu' for taxpayer assistance.

items.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
items.summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages. Following a recent surge, maize planting is forecasted to decline, as farmers shift towards crops that are less fertilizer-dependent and lower in risk amid unpredictable weather conditions.

items.title: Global turmoil is India’s 'wake-up call' to cut import dependence: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urges domestic industry to embrace self-reliance amid global challenges. He calls for increased productivity and better use of trade agreements. The minister emphasizes reducing import dependence to bolster the economy. He also highlights the potential of electric vehicles and advanced technologies like AI for growth. This presents a significant opportunity for India to emerge stronger.

items.title: China urges WTO to set up panel in case against India's support measures for solar, IT sectors
items.summary: China has asked the World Trade Organization to form a panel. This is due to a dispute with India over support for solar cells, modules, and IT sectors. Bilateral talks failed to find a solution. China claims India's measures discriminate against its goods. This follows a similar request in January regarding auto and battery incentives.

items.title: Policy planners outline India’s economic response after PM’s call for energy discipline
items.summary: At the Confederation of Indian Industry’s annual business summit on Tuesday, petroleum minister Hardeep Singh Puri pointed to the stress on state-owned fuel retailers from the global energy shock, which chief economic adviser V. Anantha Nageswaran said poses a 'live macroeconomic stress test'.

items.title: Rupee sinks to a record low
items.summary: The Indian currency closed 32 paise down at an all time low of 95.6275 per US Dollar. Intraday, it fell to a record low 95.7375 per dollar, surpassing the previous low of 95.4325

items.summary: Its induction cooktop category recorded strong momentum during the quarter, with value growth of 89 per cent and volume growth of 67 per cent

items.summary: The RBI has asked the Commissioner for Cooperation and Registrar of Cooperative Societies, Maharashtra, to issue an order for the winding up of the bank and to appoint a liquidator

items.summary: Complainant alleges absence of trustee resolution approving the transfer of 833 shares to Naval H Tata in 1989, asks successors to “make good for the loss”

items.title: Planing 500 delegations, outreach to 1,600 industry chambers to boost FTA utilisation: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India is launching ambitious plans to boost its use of free trade agreements. The commerce ministry will send 500 delegations abroad and train 1,000 people. This initiative aims to help Indian businesses increase exports and attract investments. The government is committed to supporting industry growth and achieving export targets.

items.title: India has a strong economic runway for at least next 15-20 years: Citi India chief
items.summary: India offers macroeconomic resilience, political stability, demographic strength that is rare among emerging markets: Balasubramanian

items.summary: Eyes FY27 growth on merchant payments, AI

items.summary: VerSe Innovation anticipates AI to drive over 30% of monetization in a year, becoming integral to its business strategy

items.title: Canara Bank sees more money flowing into deposits amid market volatility
items.summary: Bank sharpens its focus on low-cost liabilities and high-yield retail lending

items.title: Dixon pins FY27 growth hopes on Vivo JV approval, steady mobile demand
items.summary: Dixon Technologies said the company is in advanced-stage talks with the government regarding the Vivo joint venture and remains confident that approval is only a matter of time away

items.title: Berger Paints warns of softer demand due to inflation as FY26 profit falls 4.5%
items.summary: India’s second-largest paint-maker is turning to staggered price hikes and a sports-heavy ad strategy to combat rising input costs and other disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict.

items.title: Need nuts-and-bolts reforms, industry must invest in R&D: NITI Aayog's Gauba
items.summary: Niti Aayog member Rajiv Gauba said next-generation reforms must make a clean, decisive break from the colonial mindset of distrust of citizens and punishment even for small violations to trust-based governance

items.title: Statistics ministry pushes GDP data release to June to improve accuracy
items.summary: The Statistics and Programme Implementation Ministry has changed when it releases GDP data. Provisional GDP estimates and fourth-quarter GDP data will now be released by June 7 each year. This adjustment allows for more complete data from companies and government sources. The ministry aims to improve data quality and align with global standards.

items.title: Cut waste, curb imports: Piyush Goyal’s ‘wake-up call’ to Indian industry
items.summary: Goyal said India need to be become more efficient, productive and self-reliant the current global geopolitical and economic environment.

items.title: ‘It is coming, and it is coming big’: Uday Kotak on oil price shock
items.summary: Uday Kotak has cautioned that the impact of surging oil prices from the West Asia war is coming soon. He stresses the importance of preparation and proactive measures, as India has maintained stable fuel prices despite global increases, which may soon alter.

items.title: IMD to forecast monsoon arrival at block level to help farmers plan sowing
items.summary: IMD is launching an AI-powered block-level monsoon forecasting system covering 3,000+ sub-districts across 16 states, giving farmers 10-day advance notice of rainfall arrival.&nbsp;

items.title: ‘Substantial’ gap between what FTAs promise and what regulations currently permit, CEA Nageswaran warns
items.summary: The Chief Economic Advisor’s warning comes days after the EU’s Ambassador to India made similar statements about compliances not being used as trade barriers.

items.title: Investors continue selling as PM Modi signals crisis readiness
items.summary: The market rout was broad-based, with 2,750 stocks declining on the Nifty 50 and just 590 advancing

items.title: Govt may have to eventually decide how long OMCs can keep incurring losses: Puri
items.summary: Addressing the annual general meeting of industry lobby CII, Puri said that oil marketing companies were losing  ₹1,000 crore a day due to stagnant pump prices despite a sharp rise in international crude oil and natural gas prices.

items.title: Syrma SGS Technology expects 30% revenue growth in tricky FY27, MD Jasbir Singh Gujral says
items.summary: Syrma SGS Technology expects its automotive and medical tech verticals to remain resilient, as the order book grows steadily, MD Jasbir Singh Gujral says.

items.title: At ₹1.98 lakh crore, PSBs clock record high net profit in FY26
items.summary: With 11% growth y-o-y, this marked the fourth consecutive year of aggregate profitability for PSBs

items.title: West Asia crisis a ‘live stress test’ for India’s balance of payments, says CEA V Anantha Nageswaran
items.summary: India’s chief economic adviser warns that soaring energy costs and freight rates are no longer just foreign policy concerns, but a real-time challenge to India’s economic stability.

items.title: Moody's cuts India's 2026 growth forecast to 6% on high energy costs amid West Asia war
items.summary: India remains particularly vulnerable to high oil prices, given its dependence on imported crude oil and liquefied natural gas, Moody’s said

items.title: Retail inflation climbs to 13-month high of 3.5% in April on higher food, restaurant prices
items.summary: Data on the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for April 2026 showed that inflation quickened from the 3.4% registered in March 2026, the first month following the start of the war in West Asia

items.title: India revises GDP release calendar, annual and Q4 figures now due on June 7
items.summary: India's National Statistics Office has revised the annual GDP data release schedule, moving it from the last working day of May to June 7 annually. This change, effective from FY26 data, aims to enhance the quality and robustness of national accounts. The first revised release will be on June 5, 2026, due to a weekend holiday.

items.title: Petroleum Minister does not rule out fuel price hike amidst concerns about mounting OMCs’ losses
items.summary: Speaking at the CII Annual Business Summit 2026, Petroleum Minister Hardeep Puri said India has 60 days of crude oil and LNG stock, and 45 days of rolling stock of LPG

items.title: Quality standards key to manufacturing push; toy sector turned net exporter after QCOs: Consumer Affairs Secretary Khare
items.summary: India is staking its manufacturing ambition on quality compliance over cost, with QCOs now covering 723 products and the toy sector cited as a conversion success.&nbsp;

items.title: India-Oman FTA likely to come into force from June 1: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: India and Oman are set to implement their free trade agreement on June 1, 2026. This pact will grant India duty-free access to 98 percent of its exports to Oman. Meanwhile, India is actively pursuing a comprehensive economic partnership with Chile. The focus is on securing critical minerals, vital for India's electronics and auto sectors.

items.title: Rani Kapur moves Supreme Court over family trust-linked board meeting
items.summary: The new application, reviewed by Mint, asked the court to restrain Kapur's widow, Priya Kapur, and other respondents from changing the management structure, appointing new directors, altering bank signatories, or taking financial decisions

items.title: One Mobikwik posts ₹4 cr profit for March quarter
items.summary: One Mobikwik had incurred a loss of ₹56 crore in the same period a year ago

items.title: SBI board approves raising up to $2 billion from overseas markets in FY27
items.summary: The bank said the fundraising will be carried out in a single or multiple tranches under Reg-S/144A through public offers or private placements of fixed or floating rate bonds denominated in US dollars or other major foreign currencies.

items.title: Reforms will continue; industry needs to spend more on R&D, shed protectionist instinct: NITI Member
items.summary: NITI Aayog Member Rajiv Gauba urged Indian industry to boost R&D investment and shed protectionism to become a global production hub. He emphasized the need for "nuts and bolts" reforms to foster trust-based governance, while industry leaders called for institutionalized, measurable reforms and clear laws.

items.title: India's retail inflation stands at 3.48% in April on higher food prices
items.summary: A&nbsp;Mint poll of 20 economists had put the median estimate for India’s retail inflation at 3.8% in April.

items.title: Niva Bupa ‘very comfortable’ with group health segment constituting around 30% of portfolio, says CFO Vishwanath M
items.summary: The standalone health insurance company believes the retail health insurance industry would grow at around 16–17% CAGR over the next few years

items.title: India's retail inflation edges up to 3.48% in April as food prices firm up
items.summary: India's retail inflation saw a slight increase to 3.48 percent in April. This rise was primarily due to higher food prices. However, the overall inflation rate remains comfortably below the Reserve Bank of India's target of 4 percent. Food inflation itself climbed to 4.20 percent. Personal care and miscellaneous goods experienced the highest inflation.

items.title: It’s time to tokenize sovereign debt now that India’s e-rupee is ready to help finance evolve
items.summary: With RBI's e-rupee ready, assets can be tokenized for trading on the same ledger system that underpins this CBDC. This speed transactions up, reduces risk and allows markets to emerge. It could put G-Secs into household portfolios.

items.title: ‘Not a cost-cutting exercise’: CEO Bill Staples as GitLab plans to cut jobs, reinvest in AI agents
items.summary: ‘We must revisit the size of staffing for each role to ensure we are optimizing for speed and customer outcomes,’ Staples said.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why the GST Council plans to review taxation of ride-hailing app companies
items.summary: As ride-hailing companies push for GST exemptions on customer payments, the Goods and Services Tax Council will review existing laws to ensure clarity and uniformity in taxation.

items.title: India's bullet train project: Will Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw's 'nobody will fly' remark turn out to be true?
items.summary: One of the important factors in flying has been saving time, which will not only be matched but also bettered by the bullet train project. However, the challenge would be the location of the station and the connectivity to work areas.

items.title: Conditions favourable for onset of southwest monsoon over South Bay of Bengal, Andaman: IMD
items.summary: The southwest monsoon is set to arrive over the Bay of Bengal and Andaman Islands by the week's end. This marks a timely start to India's crucial rainfall season. However, the India Meteorological Department forecasts below-average rainfall for the country. The monsoon's arrival is vital for agriculture, impacting crops like paddy, pulses and oilseeds.

items.title: West Bengal to join Digital Agriculture Mission soon
items.summary: West Bengal is set to join the Centre’s Digital Agriculture Mission after the BJP government took charge, enabling farmer integration into AgriStack. The move aims to improve subsidy delivery, crop monitoring, insurance, credit access and data-driven policymaking for millions of farmers.

items.title: MAD politics: divisive talk on social media has pushed US Congress into mutually assured dysfunction
items.summary: Americans overwhelmingly disapprove of the way Congress functions, yet US politics seems to grow ever more polarized. Research shows the role that social media may have played in worsening the rupture between Republicans and Democrats.

items.title: India declines Russian LNG under sanctions, talks continue on permitted cargoes, sources say
items.summary: India’s reluctance has left an LNG cargo from ‌Russia’s U.S.-sanctioned ⁠Portovaya plant in the Baltic Sea unable to discharge, despite indicating India as ⁠its destination in mid-April

items.title: Short drama boom sends new wave of startups knocking on VC doors
items.summary: Micro-drama startups are attracting new funding as engagement surges and investors bet on mobile-first short-form video platforms in India.

items.title: Will an oil shock and trade turmoil get in the way of India’s 2047 aim? Let’s acquire resilience against setbacks
items.summary: Estimates of the Gulf war’s blow to India’s economy spotlight the need for uncertainty buffers. To cushion itself, India must expand strategic energy reserves, speed up its clean transition and optimize its policy path in the face of tricky trade-offs.

items.title: Halfway to fragile: Why India’s deteriorating macros need strict curbs
